title: Al-Rashid's Anger against 'Abd al-Malik b. $alih and His Consequent Imprisonment
description: and His Consequent Imprisonment Abmad b. Ibrihim b. Ismail80 has mentioned that 'Abd al-Malik b. $alib had a son called 'Abd al-Rabmin, who was one of the leading figures among the people of the time and from whom 'Abd 798. The chief town of Diyar Bakr and the modem city of that name, classical Amida. See Yiqut, Mu'jam, I, 56-7; Le Strange, Lands, rob-ii; Canard, H'amddnides, 79-81; E12 s.v Diyar Bakr. iii (Canard and Cahen). 799. Ibn al-Athir, loc. cit. Boo. Chief minister, hence, a proto-vizier, to al-Mahdi, but later imprisoned by him.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 30
